jokes.text: The last time he was in between jobs, he got deep into claymation, so this should be different, hopefully.
jokes.setup: Ben has time off between jobs and is working on something 'big'
jokes.punchline: His wife's exasperated comment about his previous claymation obsession
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy that shows Leslie knows Ben's obsessive tendencies while revealing his quirky past projects

jokes.text: Oh, boy.
jokes.setup: Ben's elaborate game explanation
jokes.punchline: Leslie's understated reaction showing her concern
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction beat showing Leslie's growing concern about Ben's obsession

jokes.text: Are the cones a metaphor? Well, yes and no.
jokes.setup: Question about the game's deeper meaning
jokes.punchline: Ben's non-answer that reveals his pseudo-intellectual approach
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben moment - taking his silly game way too seriously with pretentious non-explanations

jokes.text: This is nothing, right? When do you go back to work again? Tomorrow. It's fine. I'll just throw this in the garbage.
jokes.setup: Ben realizes his elaborate game presentation fell flat
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt to downplay his obvious obsession
jokes.explanation: Great cringe comedy as Ben tries to save face while clearly devastated about his rejected masterpiece

jokes.text: Jamm for three! Ha ha! What, are you trying to build a house with those bricks?
jokes.setup: Jamm shooting baskets in chambers
jokes.punchline: His trash talk about missed shots
jokes.explanation: Standard sports trash talk that establishes Jamm's obnoxious character

jokes.text: Because the voters, to use a technical political term, gave you the stanky boot.
jokes.setup: Jamm explaining why Leslie can't make decisions
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous 'technical political term' for being voted out
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jamm dialogue - mixing pseudo-sophistication with juvenile language

jokes.text: What, you think I'm gonna use it to put a new limestone shower in my office? Yeah, now I do.
jokes.setup: Jamm sarcastically suggesting he'd misuse funds
jokes.punchline: Leslie immediately agreeing that yes, he would do exactly that
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff where Leslie's honesty undermines Jamm's rhetorical question

jokes.text: For sale. Small house. Location: Forest. It's a little wordy, don't you think?
jokes.setup: Ron's extremely minimal house listing
jokes.punchline: Tom sarcastically calling it 'wordy'
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast - Ron's minimalism vs Tom's marketing sensibilities

jokes.text: Current owner will not clean up shotgun shells or animal carcasses.
jokes.setup: Ron's house listing continues
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning shotgun shells and animal carcasses as selling points
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Ron - treating horrifying details as minor inconveniences in a real estate listing

jokes.text: Fine, I'll clean up the shotgun shells, but the carcasses remain to fertilize my berry patch.
jokes.setup: Ron agreeing to clean up
jokes.punchline: His practical reason for keeping the dead animals
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity while staying perfectly in character - Ron's practical approach to horrifying things

jokes.text: Spladow! Check it. 'Regal Meagle Realty.' 'Find your castle.'
jokes.setup: Donna revealing her real estate business
jokes.punchline: Her over-the-top branding with 'Spladow!' and castle metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Donna's entrepreneurial confidence and flair for dramatics

jokes.text: The question I always ask myself is, what kind of mogul should I be? Fashion mogul, energy drink mogul. I even thought about downhill skiing. A mogul mogul.
jokes.setup: Tom listing various mogul types he could become
jokes.punchline: The 'mogul mogul' pun playing on skiing moguls
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that fits Tom's character perfectly - always looking for the next big thing

jokes.text: A little too good. Let's see if you live up to the hype. I'm not joking.
jokes.setup: Frank saying he heard good things about Ben
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermining it with hostility and clarifying he's not joking
jokes.explanation: Creates awkward tension and establishes Frank as an antagonistic character

jokes.text: Baby, if you can still hear me, I love you. The phone is hung up.
jokes.setup: Ben talking to Ann's unborn baby
jokes.punchline: Leslie pointing out the phone is already hung up
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff showing Ben's sweet but absent-minded nature

jokes.text: Ever since I started city council, I have gone head-to-head with Jamm 112 times. He has won 56 times, I have won 56 times.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking for help with Jamm
jokes.punchline: Revealing she's been keeping an exact tally of their battles
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment - her obsessive record-keeping and competitive nature

jokes.text: The way you stood up like that, I thought it was gonna go a different way.
jokes.setup: Ben standing up dramatically after Leslie asks for help
jokes.punchline: Leslie's confusion when he immediately says no
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection comedy playing with dramatic gesture expectations

jokes.text: Two cabins.
jokes.setup: Context needed - Ron said he can't justify owning four cabins
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact correction that it's only two cabins
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron deadpan delivery revealing he has even more property than we thought

jokes.text: Plus, it doesn't have these razor-sharp perimeter defense planks sticking out of the ground.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining benefits of his new cabin
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning dangerous defensive obstacles like a normal cabin feature
jokes.explanation: Peak Ron paranoia - treating home security spikes as a standard amenity consideration

jokes.text: For the fireplace, I'm thinking fake antlers. But what kind of scarves do we want to hang off the fake antlers?
jokes.setup: Tom staging Ron's cabin
jokes.punchline: His terrible decorating ideas that completely miss the point of a rustic cabin
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Tom's complete misunderstanding of rustic aesthetics

jokes.text: Make it 5%. I don't want you getting any ideas about us being friends.
jokes.setup: Donna offering a friends and family discount
jokes.punchline: Ron rejecting friendship while accepting business relationship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron - his principled antisocial stance even extends to commission rates

jokes.text: Ron, you want to stay outside and do nothing while they make your cabin look like garbage? I'd like that very much. We can watch the shadows get longer.
jokes.setup: Donna asking if Ron wants to avoid the staging
jokes.punchline: Ron's enthusiastic agreement and his idea of entertainment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - finding joy in simple, quiet activities while others stress about materialism

jokes.text: If I'm murdered tonight, start the investigation with those two guys.
jokes.setup: Frank and Barney giving Ben a difficult case
jokes.punchline: Ben's dark humor about his coworkers' hostility
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor that shows Ben's awareness of the workplace hostility

jokes.text: No shoes, you lose. That means we should have shoes then, right? No shoes... Hmm.
jokes.setup: Jamm's confusing house rule
jokes.punchline: Leslie trying to logic out the contradictory phrasing
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay confusion that shows both Jamm's poor communication and Leslie's analytical nature

jokes.text: It's not sexual. I'm just a really big fan of her game. Really? Because in that picture, she's wearing a bikini. Yeah, thanks. Photoshopped it myself.
jokes.setup: Jamm claiming his Michelle Wie poster isn't sexual
jokes.punchline: Admitting he photoshopped her into a bikini
jokes.explanation: Great cringe setup/payoff that reveals Jamm's creepiness and poor lies

jokes.text: How sweet is this table, by the way, huh? I got it from an actual benihana. Cost me 4 grand. Worth every penny. Hai!
jokes.setup: Jamm showing off his expensive table
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous overpayment and fake Japanese exclamation
jokes.explanation: Shows Jamm's poor financial decisions and cultural insensitivity

jokes.text: Can I interest anyone in an authentic a-Japanese a-break-a-fast? Scrambled eggs eaten with chopsticks?
jokes.setup: Jamm offering breakfast
jokes.punchline: His butchered fake Japanese accent and calling scrambled eggs 'authentic Japanese breakfast'
jokes.explanation: Continues Jamm's cultural appropriation theme, showing his ignorance of Japanese culture

jokes.text: Trees are sticky, and they got bugs in them.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to open the door for pine scent
jokes.punchline: Tom's city-boy aversion to actual nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom character moment - his superficial lifestyle clashing with actual nature

jokes.text: I heard that Dave Eggers wrote a short story about this tape cassette that he found under that tree over there.
jokes.setup: Tom trying to make the cabin seem hip
jokes.punchline: His completely made-up literary connection
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist lie that shows Tom's marketing instincts and name-dropping tendencies

jokes.text: Say it again but this time I'm gonna turn the light on, like you just had an idea.
jokes.setup: Ben solves a complex case
jokes.punchline: Frank staging it for a photo op rather than appreciating the solution
jokes.explanation: Meta comedy about workplace photo ops and corporate culture artificiality

jokes.text: You eat the shells, and you throw the seeds out.
jokes.setup: Jamm serving 'authentic edamame'
jokes.punchline: His completely backwards instructions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jamm moment showing his fake cultural knowledge and ignorance

jokes.text: How about if in the Jamm zone, there's no married chicks allowed? Caution, Jeremy. I think there are people that will find the 'No Married Chicks' zone both morally repugnant and hard to enforce.
jokes.setup: Jamm's inappropriate park zone suggestion
jokes.punchline: Chris's diplomatic but firm shutdown
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Jamm's crudeness met with Chris's professional diplomatic language

jokes.text: Ugh, Lezzie. Come on, I'm getting bored.
jokes.setup: Long negotiation session
jokes.punchline: Jamm's inappropriate nickname and childish impatience
jokes.explanation: Shows Jamm's inappropriate behavior and immaturity, though the nickname is problematic

jokes.text: Oh, no, no, no. I'm Sandra Dee. That's more of my register. You're Zuko.
jokes.setup: Karaoke song assignment
jokes.punchline: Jamm insisting on playing the female role because of his vocal range
jokes.explanation: Unexpected character moment that's both absurd and oddly endearing

jokes.text: I would like to buy five DVDs of this. No, 20. No, 60! No, that's insane. 20.
jokes.setup: Ben watching the karaoke performance
jokes.punchline: His escalating excitement then self-correction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation comedy showing Ben's enthusiasm and immediate self-awareness

jokes.text: You're describing a hotel.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining glamping with heated tents, catered meals, wifi
jokes.punchline: Ron's matter-of-fact observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron deadpan cutting through modern marketing nonsense with simple logic

jokes.text: You want to be Lex Luthor? Uh, yeah. Lex Luthor is rich.
jokes.setup: Jamm comparing himself to Lex Luthor
jokes.punchline: His practical reason for wanting to be the villain
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jamm's materialistic worldview

jokes.text: And by the way, you should know, you don't make sushi rolls out of tuna salad-- Maybe you don't.
jokes.setup: Leslie criticizing Jamm's fake Asian food
jokes.punchline: Jamm's defensive non-denial
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff showing Jamm's stubborn refusal to admit he's wrong

jokes.text: Ann arbor sounds disgusting.
jokes.setup: Learning Ann is moving to Ann Arbor
jokes.punchline: Leslie's petty criticism of the city name
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's emotional reaction through petty criticism

jokes.text: I mean, the stupid state is split up into two pieces! It's ridiculous.
jokes.setup: Leslie's rant about Michigan
jokes.punchline: Her complaint about Michigan's geographic uniqueness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie grasping for reasons to hate Michigan

jokes.text: You probably watch Million Dollar Baby and root for the stool.
jokes.setup: Leslie attacking Jamm's character
jokes.punchline: Accusing him of rooting for the tragic accident
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy insult that's both specific and devastatingly character-appropriate

jokes.text: I haven't seen it. Not a big Morgan Freeman guy. I find his voice very grating.
jokes.setup: Leslie's elaborate Million Dollar Baby insult
jokes.punchline: Jamm's complete miss of the reference and inexplicable Morgan Freeman criticism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jamm response showing his cultural obliviousness and contrarian opinions

jokes.text: Wait a second. That's not pizza. Those are calzones! I love calzones!
jokes.setup: Surprise party with 'pizza'
jokes.punchline: Ben's excitement about calzones
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Ben's calzone obsession from earlier episodes

jokes.text: Those are calzones! I love calzones! We know!
jokes.setup: Ben's coworkers surprising him with food
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top excitement about calzones and their knowing response
jokes.explanation: Solid callback to Ben's well-established character trait, showing his coworkers understand him

jokes.text: Well, it would be even faster if the governor would return my calls about my Pawnee-to-Ann arbor bullet train idea.
jokes.setup: Leslie acknowledging the distance to Michigan
jokes.punchline: Her revelation about an elaborate and impractical infrastructure project
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment showing her over-the-top solutions and political ambitions

jokes.text: I bought that cabin 18 years ago for $2,200. I do not care about the money.
jokes.setup: High offer for Ron's cabin
jokes.punchline: Revealing his incredibly low original purchase price
jokes.explanation: Shows Ron's practical nature and how long he's owned the property

jokes.text: I'd go up there and take Andy and Champion and just get away from everyone else and look at spiders.
jokes.setup: April explaining what she'd do with Ron's cabin
jokes.punchline: Her characteristically dark and antisocial but simple plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect April character moment - antisocial but genuine, with her weird fascination with spiders

jokes.text: $8, a bunch of loose cough drops, and Larry's asthma inhaler.
jokes.setup: April's offer for the cabin
jokes.punchline: The pathetic contents of her purse including someone else's medical device
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor showing April's disorganization and casual theft

jokes.text: Larry, this is part of a real estate transaction now. You have no legal claim to it.
jokes.setup: Larry recognizing his inhaler
jokes.punchline: Tom treating the inhaler as legitimate collateral
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist escalation treating a medical emergency as business

jokes.text: I will give you everything in my purse. $8, a bunch of loose cough drops, and Larry's asthma inhaler.
jokes.setup: April making an offer on the expensive cabin
jokes.punchline: Her pathetic financial resources including stolen medical equipment
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor - the complete mismatch between offer and asking price, plus Larry's inhaler

jokes.text: Jamm is gonna be so pissed when he finds out that you're leaving and your I.O.U.s are worthless.
jokes.setup: Ben making a deal with five IOUs
jokes.punchline: Revealing the IOUs will be worthless when he leaves
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff revealing Ben's clever scheme to help Leslie

jokes.text: You can play with one warrior, but it's just not nearly as good.
jokes.setup: Ben leaving his game as a gift
jokes.punchline: His note still obsessing over game mechanics
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Ben still can't help but obsess over his game details

jokes.text: I call ledgerman! No.
jokes.setup: The accountants about to play Ben's game
jokes.punchline: Fighting over the mundane scorekeeper role Ben highlighted
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback ending - the boring ledgerman role is actually desirable to accountants
